diseases.name: Asthma
diseases.summary: Asthma is a chronic respiratory disease characterized by variable airflow obstruction and airway inflammation. GENARCH presents asthma as a population-level gene–environment system (educational only; not medical advice).
diseases.adolescent_relevance: Asthma commonly affects adolescents and young adults and is sensitive to environmental exposures in population studies.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS concepts are discussed for education only; GENARCH does not provide individual risk prediction.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Oxidative stress and inflammatory signaling in airway epithelium.
Airway irritation context in population studies.
Developmental programming context in cohorts.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Many asthma GWAS have been European-ancestry dominated historically.
population_equity.transferability_notes: Transferability varies across ancestries due to LD and cohort composition.
population_equity.data_gaps: GxE evidence is sparse for many exposures and populations.

references.journal: Journal of the Air & Waste Management Association

diseases.name: Coronary Artery Disease
diseases.summary: Coronary artery disease reflects atherosclerotic narrowing of coronary arteries. GENARCH summarizes population-level genetic architecture and exposure modifiers for education only.
diseases.adolescent_relevance: CAD events occur later in life, but exposure and cardiometabolic trajectories begin earlier; GENARCH content is educational only.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS is discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not project individual risk.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Diet patterns influence LDL metabolism and inflammation.
Smoking is strongly associated with CAD burden in cohorts.
Ambient particulates are associated with cardiovascular outcomes in population studies.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Many large CAD GWAS have been European-ancestry dominated historically.
population_equity.transferability_notes: PRS performance can vary across ancestries due to LD and cohort composition.
population_equity.data_gaps: Exposure measurement differs across settings; GxE evidence is incomplete.

diseases.name: Type 2 Diabetes
diseases.summary: Type 2 diabetes is characterized by chronic hyperglycemia due to insulin resistance and beta-cell dysfunction. GENARCH provides population-level educational summaries.
diseases.adolescent_relevance: Adolescent/young adult onset is increasing in some settings; GENARCH content is educational only.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS is discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Diet patterns influence insulin resistance and glucose homeostasis.
Lower activity is associated with insulin resistance in cohorts.
Evidence is heterogeneous and compound-specific.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: T2D GWAS increasingly include multi-ancestry cohorts, but representation remains uneven.
population_equity.transferability_notes: PRS performance can vary by ancestry and exposure context.
population_equity.data_gaps: Exposure and phenotype measurement heterogeneity limits inference.

diseases.name: Hypertension
diseases.summary: Hypertension is persistently elevated blood pressure influenced by kidney, vascular, endocrine, and autonomic regulation. GENARCH provides population-level educational summaries.
diseases.adolescent_relevance: Blood pressure trajectories can begin in adolescence; content is educational only.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS is discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Sodium intake influences fluid balance and blood pressure regulation pathways.
Stress-related neuroendocrine signaling can influence vascular tone in cohorts.
Lower activity is associated with cardiometabolic factors linked to blood pressure.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Blood pressure GWAS have historically been European-ancestry weighted.
population_equity.transferability_notes: PRS performance can vary by ancestry and measurement context.
population_equity.data_gaps: Dietary sodium measurement and confounding vary across cohorts.

diseases.name: Breast Cancer
diseases.summary: Breast cancer is a heterogeneous malignancy with hereditary high-penetrance variants and polygenic background. GENARCH provides educational population-level summaries.
diseases.adolescent_relevance: Rare in adolescence; included for educational oncogenetics and inheritance context only.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions or thresholds.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Alcohol is associated with breast cancer risk in observational studies.
DNA damage context; depends on dose and timing.
Evidence is heterogeneous and compound-specific.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Early breast cancer GWAS were European-ancestry dominated; representation is improving.
population_equity.transferability_notes: PRS transferability varies across ancestries and subtypes.
population_equity.data_gaps: Subtype and exposure measurement heterogeneity limit generalization.

diseases.name: Colorectal Cancer
diseases.summary: Colorectal cancer arises from colon/rectum epithelium and involves hereditary syndromes and polygenic background. GENARCH provides educational population-level summaries.
diseases.adolescent_relevance: Rare in adolescence; hereditary syndromes illustrate mechanism and inheritance in an educational framing.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Diet patterns influence metabolic/inflammatory context in cohorts.
Microbiome patterns correlate with CRC; causality is complex.
Alcohol is associated with cancer outcomes in population studies.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: CRC GWAS have been European-ancestry dominated historically; representation improving.
population_equity.transferability_notes: PRS performance varies by ancestry and screening context.
population_equity.data_gaps: Microbiome study heterogeneity and confounding limit inference.

diseases.name: Lung Cancer
diseases.summary: Lung cancer risk is strongly influenced by inhaled exposures (especially smoking) and has gene–environment components in population studies. GENARCH provides educational summaries only.
diseases.adolescent_relevance: Rare in adolescence; included to illustrate exposure-driven carcinogenesis and GxE reasoning at a population level.

top_loci.evidence: Nicotine receptor locus; exposure mediation and interaction are complex

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Smoking introduces carcinogens and promotes chronic inflammation and DNA damage context.
Ambient particulates associated with lung cancer outcomes in some studies.
Occupational particulates are context-dependent inhaled exposures.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Many lung cancer GWAS have been European-ancestry dominated historically.
population_equity.transferability_notes: PRS and locus mapping can vary with ancestry and exposure distribution.
population_equity.data_gaps: Separating genetic effects from exposure mediation is challenging.

diseases.name: Schizophrenia
diseases.summary: Schizophrenia is a neuropsychiatric disorder with highly polygenic architecture and environmental context studied at the population level. GENARCH provides educational summaries only.
diseases.adolescent_relevance: Typical onset is late adolescence/early adulthood; GENARCH avoids stigmatizing language and provides population-level evidence only.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Stress exposure associations are complex and confounded in cohorts.
Cannabis associations with psychosis outcomes depend on context; causality remains debated.
Sleep disruption associations are often bidirectional and context-dependent.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Large schizophrenia GWAS have been European-ancestry dominated historically.
population_equity.transferability_notes: PRS performance varies across ancestries due to LD and cohort composition.
population_equity.data_gaps: Exposure measurement and ascertainment vary across settings.

diseases.name: Major Depressive Disorder
diseases.summary: Major depressive disorder is a common condition with moderate heritability and strong environmental context in population studies. GENARCH provides educational summaries only.
diseases.adolescent_relevance: Often emerges in adolescence/young adulthood; GENARCH avoids individualized risk or clinical advice.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Stress exposure is associated with depression outcomes in cohorts; causal direction is complex.
Sleep disruption is associated with mental health outcomes; bidirectionality is common.
Associations vary and can be confounded by multiple factors.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Large MDD GWAS have been European-ancestry dominated historically.
population_equity.transferability_notes: PRS performance varies across ancestries due to LD and phenotype definitions.
population_equity.data_gaps: Exposure measurement heterogeneity and confounding are substantial.

diseases.adolescent_relevance: Typically manifests later in life; included to illustrate neuroinflammation and genetic architecture concepts in an educational framing.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Sleep patterns are associated with cognitive outcomes in population studies; causal direction is complex.
Air pollution is studied in relation to neurocognitive outcomes; evidence varies by design.
Vascular and cardiometabolic factors influence brain health in cohorts.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Many AD genetics studies are European-ancestry dominated historically.

diseases.name: Multiple Sclerosis
diseases.summary: Multiple sclerosis is an immune-mediated CNS disease with strong HLA associations and environmental context in population studies. GENARCH provides educational summaries only.
diseases.adolescent_relevance: Often begins in young adulthood; earlier-life exposures are discussed in population research.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Vitamin D status proxies are associated with MS risk in some studies; confounding varies.
Smoking is associated with MS outcomes in some cohorts.
Infection exposures are studied as modifiers; pathogen-specific evidence is complex.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Large MS GWAS have been European-ancestry dominated historically.

diseases.adolescent_relevance: Typically diagnosed later in life; included to illustrate exposure-driven lung remodeling mechanisms at a population level.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Smoking is a dominant population-level driver of COPD burden.
Ambient particulates can contribute to chronic airway irritation in cohorts.
Occupational particulates contribute in some settings, often interacting with smoking.
